title: if/then/else expression
manager: `if/then/else` as an expression that yields a value, not a statement that performs an effect — so a guard or an assignment can branch inline and every branch is required to produce a value of the same type. This is the functional shape that keeps FSL expressions total and typed, instead of leaking the host language's statement-oriented control flow into machine logic where it can't be reasoned about.
cs: expression-form if/then/else; both branches required, unified result type; totality-preserving.
